title: NSFAS advances 2026 funding cycle with significant approvals and recoveries
bodyText:
[{"type":"intro_bold","text":"NSFAS stressed its commitment to fair, transparent, and efficient processing, advising applicants to keep contact details current and monitor official communications."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"NATIONAL NEWS - The National Student Financial Aid Scheme (NSFAS) continues to serve as a cornerstone in broadening access to post-school education and training for South African youth.","html":"NATIONAL NEWS - The National Student Financial Aid Scheme (NSFAS) continues to serve as a cornerstone in broadening access to post-school education and training for South African youth."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"As a government entity, NSFAS provides crucial financial support to eligible students at public universities and Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) colleges, removing financial obstacles that might otherwise hinder talented individuals from disadvantaged backgrounds in pursuing higher education.","html":"As a government entity, NSFAS provides crucial financial support to eligible students at public universities and Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) colleges, removing financial obstacles that might otherwise hinder talented individuals from disadvantaged backgrounds in pursuing higher education."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"By investing in human capital development through its bursary and loan programmes, NSFAS targets students from poor and working-class families, as well as those in the \"missing middle\" income bracket who fall outside full bursary eligibility but still face significant barriers to affording tertiary studies.","html":"By investing in human capital development through its bursary and loan programmes, NSFAS targets students from poor and working-class families, as well as those in the \"missing middle\" income bracket who fall outside full bursary eligibility but still face significant barriers to affording tertiary studies."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The scheme emphasises efficient, transparent allocation of resources in line with national policy priorities.","html":"The scheme emphasises efficient, transparent allocation of resources in line with national policy priorities."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"In a significant development bolstering its financial capacity, NSFAS has welcomed the Special Investigating Unit's (SIU) recovery of over R1.7 billion in unallocated or improperly held funds from various higher education institutions, TVET colleges, and unqualified former beneficiaries.","html":"In a significant development bolstering its financial capacity, NSFAS has welcomed the Special Investigating Unit's (SIU) recovery of over R1.7 billion in unallocated or improperly held funds from various higher education institutions, TVET colleges, and unqualified former beneficiaries."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"These recovered funds, part of a broader effort exceeding R2 billion in total recoveries, will be redirected to support thousands of students' educational journeys at public institutions.","html":"These recovered funds, part of a broader effort exceeding R2 billion in total recoveries, will be redirected to support thousands of students' educational journeys at public institutions."},{"type":"heading","text":"Progress on the 2026 application cycle","level":"h3"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"NSFAS has provided key updates on the processing of applications for the 2026 academic year, covering first-time entering students (FTEN), returning university students, and continuing TVET college students.","html":"NSFAS has provided key updates on the processing of applications for the 2026 academic year, covering first-time entering students (FTEN), returning university students, and continuing TVET college students."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"For first-time entering students, NSFAS has approved funding for 660,039 applicants this week, an increase from the previous week's figure of 632,333, reflecting steady progress in expanding access."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"A further 85,662 applications are in progress as verification of outstanding documentation continues, while 21,483 remain pending due to missing supporting documents. NSFAS has urged affected students to submit required materials promptly to prevent delays.","html":"A further 85,662 applications are in progress as verification of outstanding documentation continues, while 21,483 remain pending due to missing supporting documents. NSFAS has urged affected students to submit required materials promptly to prevent delays."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Of the 116,266 applications that did not qualify for bursary funding, 13,052 applicants have been offered NSFAS loans as an alternative pathway to support. Among returning university students, 436,924 have satisfied academic progression requirements and remain eligible for continued funding.","html":"Of the 116,266 applications that did not qualify for bursary funding, 13,052 applicants have been offered NSFAS loans as an alternative pathway to support. Among returning university students, 436,924 have satisfied academic progression requirements and remain eligible for continued funding."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"However, 109,761 did not meet these standards and are ineligible for further support.","html":"However, 109,761 did not meet these standards and are ineligible for further support."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"NSFAS highlighted concerns over 4,945 pending applications due to outstanding academic results not yet submitted by universities, calling on institutions to accelerate submissions for timely decisions.","html":"NSFAS highlighted concerns over 4,945 pending applications due to outstanding academic results not yet submitted by universities, calling on institutions to accelerate submissions for timely decisions."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"For continuing TVET college students, 210,989 are engaged in the funding process.","html":"For continuing TVET college students, 210,989 are engaged in the funding process."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Of these, 127,503 have met progression criteria and qualify for ongoing support, while 79,461 do not.","html":"Of these, 127,503 have met progression criteria and qualify for ongoing support, while 79,461 do not."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Additional cases include 1,407 instances of course mismatch requiring resolution, 2,126 awaiting funding decisions, and 811 in the process of linkage to appropriate funding streams.","html":"Additional cases include 1,407 instances of course mismatch requiring resolution, 2,126 awaiting funding decisions, and 811 in the process of linkage to appropriate funding streams."},{"type":"heading","text":"Enrolment and registration insights","level":"h3"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Projected registrations for 2026 include 106,615 first-time entering students at TVET colleges and 161,122 at universities.","html":"Projected registrations for 2026 include 106,615 first-time entering students at TVET colleges and 161,122 at universities."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"To date, NSFAS has received registration data for 44,616 TVET students and 2,461 university students, with ongoing efforts to secure prompt submissions from institutions for efficient funding disbursement.","html":"To date, NSFAS has received registration data for 44,616 TVET students and 2,461 university students, with ongoing efforts to secure prompt submissions from institutions for efficient funding disbursement."},{"type":"heading","text":"NSFAS loan programme developments","level":"h3"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The NSFAS loan scheme supports \"missing middle\" students whose family incomes exceed bursary thresholds but remain insufficient for full self-funding. Loans feature favourable terms, including low interest rates and flexible post-graduation repayment.","html":"The NSFAS loan scheme supports \"missing middle\" students whose family incomes exceed bursary thresholds but remain insufficient for full self-funding. Loans feature favourable terms, including low interest rates and flexible post-graduation repayment."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"For the 2026 cycle, NSFAS received 26,538 loan applications. Of these, 4,609 met the criteria, and 1,561 have been approved to date. Around 20,368 did not qualify due to academic or financial ineligibility.","html":"For the 2026 cycle, NSFAS received 26,538 loan applications. Of these, 4,609 met the criteria, and 1,561 have been approved to date. Around 20,368 did not qualify due to academic or financial ineligibility."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The scheme encouraged unsuccessful applicants to pursue academic improvement and consider future applications.","html":"The scheme encouraged unsuccessful applicants to pursue academic improvement and consider future applications."},{"type":"heading","text":"Appeals process update","level":"h3"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The appeals mechanism allows students to seek review of adverse outcomes when new information emerges or circumstances warrant reconsideration.","html":"The appeals mechanism allows students to seek review of adverse outcomes when new information emerges or circumstances warrant reconsideration."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"For 2026, NSFAS received 91,937 appeals, with the majority (78,347 or 85.22%) from continuing students, followed by 8,533 (9.28%) from first-time entrants and 5,057 (5.5%) from seniors.","html":"For 2026, NSFAS received 91,937 appeals, with the majority (78,347 or 85.22%) from continuing students, followed by 8,533 (9.28%) from first-time entrants and 5,057 (5.5%) from seniors."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Progress includes:","html":"Progress includes:"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"10,445 appeals approved, enabling funding for these students.5,407 rejected due to insufficient criteria or information.44,983 finalised, withdrawn, deleted, or closed per policy.3,209 awaiting supporting documents—applicants are urged to submit promptly.27,893 still under review by the NSFAS Appeals Committee.","html":"
  • 10,445 appeals approved, enabling funding for these students.
  • 5,407 rejected due to insufficient criteria or information.
  • 44,983 finalised, withdrawn, deleted, or closed per policy.
  • 3,209 awaiting supporting documents—applicants are urged to submit promptly.
  • 27,893 still under review by the NSFAS Appeals Committee.
    • "},{"type":"paragraph","text":"NSFAS stressed its commitment to fair, transparent, and efficient processing, advising applicants to keep contact details current and monitor official communications.","html":"NSFAS stressed its commitment to fair, transparent, and efficient processing, advising applicants to keep contact details current and monitor official communications."},{"type":"heading","text":"Student accommodation efforts","level":"h3"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Recognising accommodation's role in academic success and well-being, NSFAS collaborates with students, institutions, the South African Union of Students (SAUS), and the South African TVET Student Association (SAVETSA) to refine processes.","html":"Recognising accommodation's role in academic success and well-being, NSFAS collaborates with students, institutions, the South African Union of Students (SAUS), and the South African TVET Student Association (SAVETSA) to refine processes."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"A guidance circular has been issued to institutions, clarifying procedures. Private accommodation rates for 2026 are under review, informed by inflation, progression data, enrolment projections and, budget allocations announcements will follow finalisation.","html":"A guidance circular has been issued to institutions, clarifying procedures. Private accommodation rates for 2026 are under review, informed by inflation, progression data, enrolment projections and, budget allocations announcements will follow finalisation."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"For the current cycle, NSFAS has received 194,069 accommodation applications (78,409 from TVET colleges and 115,662 from universities)."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Approvals stand at 16,526 for TVET and 39,127 for universities, with many pending institutional or landlord review.In response to challenges, including at institutions like Cape Peninsula University of Technology (CPUT), NSFAS is advancing a transitional framework toward centralised, transparent management.","html":"Approvals stand at 16,526 for TVET and 39,127 for universities, with many pending institutional or landlord review.In response to challenges, including at institutions like Cape Peninsula University of Technology (CPUT), NSFAS is advancing a transitional framework toward centralised, transparent management."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"This includes aligning accommodation timelines with the academic calendar and upgrading the accommodation portal for applications, status tracking, offer acceptance, and maintenance reporting, integrated with institutional systems.","html":"This includes aligning accommodation timelines with the academic calendar and upgrading the accommodation portal for applications, status tracking, offer acceptance, and maintenance reporting, integrated with institutional systems."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"NSFAS reaffirmed its dedication to equitable access, efficient resource use, and stakeholder collaboration.","html":"NSFAS reaffirmed its dedication to equitable access, efficient resource use, and stakeholder collaboration."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Students and partners are encouraged to use official channels for updates and submit outstanding information swiftly to support a successful 2026 academic year.","html":"Students and partners are encouraged to use official channels for updates and submit outstanding information swiftly to support a successful 2026 academic year."}]

images.alt: NSFAS advances 2026 funding cycle with significant approvals and recoveries

author: NSFAS
teaser: NSFAS stressed its commitment to fair, transparent, and efficient processing, advising applicants to keep contact details current and monitor official communications.
featuredCaption: NSFAS reaffirmed its dedication to equitable access, efficient resource use, and stakeholder collaboration.  Image: File image
